Klaviyo API pricing access free trial developer
Klaviyo free plan API key developer access self-serve
Klaviyo API reference endpoints REST API documentation
site:developers.klaviyo.com API reference endpoints profiles campaigns lists segments

researched_category.notes: Klaviyo is a data-driven email and SMS marketing platform primarily for ecommerce brands.

one_liner.value: Data-driven email and SMS marketing automation platform for ecommerce businesses.
one_liner.notes: Official site and developer portal both describe it as a marketing platform for personalized experiences.

methods.notes: Klaviyo provides 3 auth methods: (1) Private API key (header-based, primary for server-side), (2) OAuth2 for app marketplace integrations, (3) Public key / Site ID for client-side endpoints only. The private API key uses a custom header format 'Authorization: Klaviyo-API-Key {key}' which isn't standard Bearer token, hence 'custom' is included.

access.notes: Free plan exists indefinitely (up to 250 profiles, 500 emails/month). API keys can be created from Settings > API Keys on any plan including free. You need Owner/Admin/Manager role to manage keys but that's a role within your own account, not a gate.

summary.value: Klaviyo has a comprehensive, well-documented REST API (JSON:API compliant) covering Profiles, Lists, Segments, Campaigns, Flows, Templates, Metrics, Events, Catalogs, Coupons, Tags, and more. OpenAPI spec and Postman collections are available. SDKs exist for Python, PHP, Ruby, and Node. The API supports advanced filtering, sorting, sparse fieldsets, cursor-based pagination, and relationships.
summary.notes: The API is versioned and documented at developers.klaviyo.com with both server-side (/api) and client-side (/client) endpoints.

agent_interface.notes: Klaviyo maintains an official MCP server at mcp.klaviyo.com/mcp with OAuth authentication. It offers ~40 core tools (or more). Also available on Composio toolkit (225 tools) and StackOne (172 actions). Community MCP servers exist on GitHub (e.g., mattcoatsworth/Klaviyo-MCP-Server). SDKs (Python, Node, PHP, Ruby) are officially maintained.

rationale.value: Klaviyo is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. It has a well-documented public REST API with broad coverage, a free self-serve tier that includes API key generation, and an official MCP server hosted by Klaviyo at mcp.klaviyo.com/mcp. Multiple community MCP servers and Composio/StackOne integrations already exist. The only minor friction is that API key management requires Owner/Admin/Manager role, but this is a standard account permission not an external gate.

s1.quote: Create apps and integrations using Klaviyo's API that help your business grow. Get started for free.
s1.title: Klaviyo Developers: API Reference & Technical Guides

s2.quote: Klaviyo pricing: Start with a free plan. Explore Klaviyo's free plan—get started at no cost with email marketing sends, text message credits, Customer Hub, and built-in reporting.
s2.title: Klaviyo Pricing | Start Free with Email & SMS

s3.quote: Klaviyo provides 3 methods of authentication including private key authentication and OAuth (used to call server-side APIs) and public key authentication (used to call client-side APIs).
s3.title: Authenticate API requests

s4.quote: Your app must use OAuth in order to be considered for Klaviyo's App Marketplace.
s4.title: Make API calls using OAuth

s5.quote: Only Owners or Admins can access the API keys tab.
s5.title: How to manage your account's API keys

s6.quote: Free plan highlights: Up to 250 profiles, 500 emails/month, 150 mobile message credits, 10,000 Composer credits, Built-in reporting, Email support.
s6.title: Klaviyo Pricing | Start Free with Email & SMS

s7.quote: You can obtain and make changes to your API keys from your account's Settings page under the API Keys tab. To manage API keys, you must have an Owner, Admin, or Manager role on the account.
s7.title: Obtain API credentials

s8.quote: Klaviyo's modern APIs offer powerful functionality via the 'relationships' object, a thorough set of syntax offered by JSON:API for modeling relationships between resources.
s8.title: API overview

s9.quote: Install one of our new SDKs. Make test calls with the new API collection in our Postman workspace.
s9.title: API overview

s10.quote: Lists API overview. Profiles API. Tags API. Campaigns API overview. Flows API.
s10.title: Lists API overview

s11.quote: The Klaviyo Model Context Protocol (MCP) server seamlessly integrates with Klaviyo's APIs, enabling AI clients to interact with your Klaviyo data.
s11.title: Klaviyo MCP server

s12.quote: Klaviyo MCP for AI Agents - Securely connect your AI agents and chatbots (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, etc) with Klaviyo MCP or direct API.
s12.title: Klaviyo MCP Integration for AI Agents

s13.quote: A comprehensive Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for interacting with the Klaviyo API.
s13.title: mattcoatsworth/Klaviyo-MCP-Server

limitations: Could not fetch the 'Getting Started' page at developers.klaviyo.com/en/docs/getting_started (404 error). However, the authentication docs and MCP docs were successfully fetched and provide sufficient evidence.

The 'custom' auth method tag is used because the private API key uses a header format 'Authorization: Klaviyo-API-Key {key}' which is not a standard Bearer token format, though it functions like an API key.
